metric_value: More comprehensive Net development vision than competition: dual-track traditional + Net client-server
notes: Aberdeen's lead assessment; competitors focused only on HTML generation

metric_value: Development tools (traditional + Net)
notes: Component 1 of 4 in REN architecture

metric_value: Database tools (traditional + Net)
notes: Component 2 of 4 in REN architecture

metric_value: Repository-based application life-cycle management tools
notes: Component 3 of 4 in REN architecture

metric_value: Reusable software components (Weblets)
notes: Component 4 of 4 in REN architecture

metric_value: Industrial-strength, complex business applications; cross-platform Windows/OS2/Mac; binary compatible
notes: Customer satisfaction data from Aberdeen field research

metric_value: TCP sockets, FTP, SMTP/POP3/MIME email, HTTP client/server, HTML generation, Java code generation
notes: Comprehensive set of Net protocols; goes beyond competitors' HTML-only

metric_value: Web Enabler demonstrated accessing FedEx parcel-tracking website and updating internal database automatically
notes: Real-world demo proving web scraping/data extraction concept in 1996

metric_value: Vista-based user/group privilege system controlling table and column-level access for web database queries
notes: Presaged modern API gateway and row-level security concepts

metric_value: Manages HTML, graphics, CGI scripts, Java applets in single version-controlled site; time-sensitive content publishing
notes: Forerunner of modern web CMS/CDN staging concepts

metric_value: Successful REN execution could propel Blyth from respected-but-small vendor to IT household name
notes: Aberdeen's core forward-looking assessment

metric_value: Never became IT household name; renamed Omnis Technology (1997), Raining Data (2000), TigerLogic (2008); sold to Cliftech Solutions in 2017
notes: Source: Wikipedia/TigerLogic; Aberdeen's breakthrough prediction did not materialize

metric_value: Aberdeen believes Blyth has a twelve month or more advantage over most competitors on Web Enabler-style functionality
notes: Parallel to Aberdeen's 12-month lead claim for Baan

metric_value: Web development middleware market commoditized by 2000; ASP.NET, PHP, Java EE dominated; Blyth's products could not scale to mainstream adoption
notes: The 12-month lead was not sustained; larger vendors (Microsoft, IBM, Sun) absorbed the market

metric_value: Word-of-mouth growth + VAR channel + early 'Net market entry; avoid HTML server and page-generation commodity; focus on mission-critical Net middleware
notes: Aberdeen endorses selective market targeting but notes weak distribution/marketing historically

metric_value: Named customers include Barclays Bank, Federal Express, Harvard University, Lockheed Martin, Northern Telecom
notes: High-value blue-chip reference customers in 1996

metric_name: Aberdeen warning on Net development maturity
metric_value: IS professionals will soon be 'up to their necks in a swamp of immature Net development products'; will favor high-end tools
source_page: Where Blyth's REN Products are Most Effective
notes: Prescient about 1990s web development chaos; dot-com boom confirmed the pattern

metric_value: Component marketplace: Blyth + customers + third parties submit components for negotiated payments — buy vs. make decision
notes: Foreshadowed modern NPM/component marketplaces concept; Blyth too early and too small to execute

metric_value: Net application development requires same rigor and tool classes as client-server; most developers not yet aware of this
notes: Core Aberdeen thesis proving prescient as enterprise web apps matured through 2000s

metric_value: Mission-critical Net applications should grow dramatically in 1997 and beyond as Net-enabled electronic commerce takes off
source_page: Where Blyth's REN Products are Most Effective
notes: Trajectory prediction was correct; Blyth just was not positioned to capitalize

metric_value: Second-generation CADE with application logic partitioning, deeper OO, templates/wizards, SQL-aware objects
notes: Planned product enhancement extending OMNIS into mid-tier architecture

metric_value: Past success inhibited by less-than-stellar distribution and marketing; must ensure Net developers discover the company
notes: Aberdeen correctly identified distribution as Blyth's key vulnerability
